title: GitHub API の 401 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article contains a significant factual error regarding Personal Access Token (PAT) expiration limits.
The guidance on Authorization header format for GitHub API is partially inaccurate and may lead to confusion.
Some factual claims, while generally true, lack direct primary source citations from GitHub's official documentation for specific error messages or general troubleshooting tips.
absolute wording may overstate technical certainty
one or more claims are unsupported or unverifiable
required_actions: Correct the information regarding PAT expiration, clarifying the distinction between classic PATs, fine-grained PATs, and organizational policies.

Revise the section on Authorization header format to accurately reflect that both 'token' and 'Bearer' are generally accepted for PATs, and 'Bearer' is required for JWTs, aligning with GitHub's official documentation.
Add more specific citations for common error messages and debugging advice, directly linking to GitHub Docs where available.
Consider adding a publication date to the article for better context on freshness.
Soften absolute claims unless they are directly sourced.
Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.

sources.title: Authenticating to the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: A 401 Unauthorized error occurs with the GitHub API when authentication for the request fails, indicating that authentication credentials are invalid.

sources.title: Authenticating to the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: If you use a REST API endpoint with invalid credentials, you will initially receive a 401 Unauthorized response.

sources.title: [BUG] GitHub Analyzer API Returning 401 Unauthorized Error · Issue #840 · S3DFX-CYBER/GSoC-Org-Finder-
sources.claim: Missing or invalid GitHub API token is a potential reason for a 401 Unauthorized error.

sources.title: Rate limits for the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: The GitHub API may return a 'Bad credentials' message as part of a 401 error.

sources.title: Managing your personal access tokens - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: A 401 error occurs if the Personal Access Token (PAT) used for GitHub API authentication expires or is deleted.

sources.title: New PAT rotation policies preview and optional expiration for fine-grained PATs
sources.claim: For fine-grained Personal Access Tokens (PATs), the maximum lifetime for organizations is typically 366 days, and developers can create fine-grained tokens with no expiration for personal projects.

sources.title: Authenticating to the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: The GitHub API generally accepts both 'Authorization: Bearer' and 'Authorization: token' for Personal Access Tokens, but 'Authorization: Bearer' must be used for JSON Web Tokens (JWT).

sources.title: Managing your personal access tokens - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: If the scope of a generated Personal Access Token (PAT) is restricted, a 401 error can occur when accessing specific endpoints due to insufficient permissions.

sources.title: Authenticating as a GitHub App installation - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: For example, private repositories cannot be accessed without the 'repo' scope.

sources.title: gh auth login
sources.claim: When using GitHub CLI or Git, a 401 error can occur if authentication information is not set in environment variables or configuration files like ~/.gitconfig.

sources.title: API Versions - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: GitHub supports multiple API versions, and requests to older versions may have different authentication requirements or behaviors.

sources.title: Getting started with the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: When using REST API v3, it is recommended to explicitly specify the version with the 'Accept' header, e.g., `Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json`.

sources.title: Forming calls with GraphQL - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: The GitHub GraphQL API uses the same Authorization header format as the REST API, but the endpoint is different (e.g., `https://api.github.com/graphql`).

sources.title: Forming calls with GraphQL - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: The same Personal Access Token (PAT) is valid when using the GraphQL API, but attention to the request format (queries vs. mutations) is necessary.

sources.title: GitHub REST API authentication
sources.claim: The provided link 'GitHub REST API authentication' is a valid official documentation page.

sources.title: GitHub API Rate Limits
sources.claim: The provided link 'GitHub API Rate Limits' is a valid official documentation page.

sources.title: Overview of the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: GitHub API response headers include `X-RateLimit-*` and `X-GitHub-Request-Id` which are useful for debugging and support inquiries.

unsupported_claims: It is one of the most frequently encountered errors when calling the GitHub API (lack of objective quantification from primary sources).

curl command for no authentication gives `{"message": "Requires authentication", "documentation_url": "https://docs.github.com/rest/reference/users#get-the-authenticated-user"}` (direct evidence for this exact message for 'no token' is scarce and contradicted by other docs suggesting 404/403 for missing tokens).
Mistakes in header name or value spacing can also cause 401 errors (general HTTP knowledge, not explicitly stated by GitHub as a distinct 401 cause for PATs).
GitHub APIを呼び出すときに最も頻繁に遭遇するエラーの一つであり、適切な認証情報を提供することで解決できます。
curlコマンドで認証情報なしでGitHub APIにアクセスした場合のメッセージ例: `{"message": "Requires authentication", "documentation_url": "https://docs.github.com/rest/reference/users#get-the-authenticated-user"}`
ヘッダー名や値のスペース配置のミスも401エラーの原因になります。
